channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bournemouth Close, 30th March
item.description:
Unacceptable and persistent amounts of rubble and commercial goods fly tipped on Bournemouth close, both in front of Peckham Palms/City Food and on the corner where an industrial carriage has been left to rot for months.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Bournemouth Close, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Timber dumped outside 34 Bramblebury Rd, 30th March
item.description:
Left there for weeks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bramblebury Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge left outside my property (not mine), 30th March
item.description:
I am reporting a fly-tipping incident outside my property in Bromley. A fridge has been left outside my home without my permission. This item does not belong to me and has been placed there by a neighbour. Details: Location: Between 133 and 135 Southlands Road Item: Fridge/freezer Responsible person: 131 Southlands Road It is unsightly and is affecting my enjoyment of my home. It also requires proper disposal due to environmental regulations. I would like this to be investigated and removed. Please confirm this has been logged and advise on next steps.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southlands Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kempis Way SE22, 30th March
item.description:
Bad weeds and overgrowth on footpath and gully on Kempis Way, requires urgent treatment. Cleaner using single hoe is not sufficient, waste of time and effort. Southwark not providing staff with suitable equipment. Improper use of taxpayer funds.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Kempis Way, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No recycling collection for 9 consecutive weeks, 30th March
item.description:
We havent receieved any recycling collection services for 9 consecutive weeks now. after continuous emails with hackneys waste operations saying 'its been escalated to the relevant teams operations' and still nothing has happened.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lanes, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A branvh hss fallrn from a tree. It msy be psttly attached., 30th March
item.description:
We think someone had possibly tried to climb an unsiitablebbrsnvh , and had damaged the tree, sadly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Court Downs Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Restaurant rubbish, 30th March
item.description:
Second week running rubbish, must include food waste, left on pavement, ravishing by foxes. Enforcement needed?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 100, Bromley Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old furniture just been dumped, 30th March
item.description:
Came back to car after walking dogs,old bedroom furniture dumped
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Erith Road, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping base of tree outside alley/1 Powerscroft road, 30th March
item.description:
Continued fly tipping by residents/businesses on Lower Clapton road with access to Powerscroft road via alleyway

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Powerscroft Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog Fouling on pavement adjacent to Willow Bank School, 30th March
item.description:
There has been an increase in dog fouling on seacourt road in particular on the pavement which runs along side willowbank school - our children have been forced to walk in the road due to the persistent issue - which encounters other safety issues - please can this be addressed and cleaned up as a matter of urgency since we are still open this week - Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Seacourt Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street Light outside No.3 Anderton Close not working., 30th March
item.description:
The street light outside No. 3 Anderton Close has not been working for several months. Please look into it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anderton Close, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Construction waste dumped, 30th March
item.description:
Large building material bag dumped on side of road
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several sections of broken or collapsed fence., 30th March
item.description:
Sections of white picket fence between parkland on Kings Hall Road and Bailes Place are broken or have collapsed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kings Hall Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped rubbish 15 Bramblebury rd, 30th March
item.description:
Rubbish on street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bramblebury Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytpping o/s 43 Pickford Lane, Bexleyheath, 30th March
item.description:
Flytpping o/s 43 Pickford Lane, Bexleyheath on f/way...3 seater sofa & cushions/childrens toys
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38, Pickford Lane, Welling, Greater London

item.title: Old bench seat dumped, 30th March
item.description:
As above

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Acer Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping - still not removed (TfL says it is Greenwich Councils responsibility., 30th March
item.description:
Fly tipping has occurred round the back of Royal Hill Court. Again. The problem has not been resolved and more continues to be dumped there. The land is technically owned by TfL but after contacting them they have stated that because it is in a publically accessible area it is the responsibility of Greenwich Council to be removed. I has pasted the email reply from TfL. Please don’t just remove this request, it is incredibly frustrating that neither the council or TfL wants to take responsibility and the pile of rubbish continues to increase. This will not go away, and soon is likely to become a significant health and safety issue if more waste including harmful substances are dumped. “Thank you for your feedback form of 27 February about the repeated fly‑tipping behind Royal Hill Court. I appreciate the impact this has had on residents and the time you have taken to document the issue. Thank you also for providing the photographs, which clearly show the extent of the fly‑tipping. These images have helped us confirm that the waste has been deposited in the publicly accessible service alley outside the fenced operational DLR area. Following your request for clarification, we have reviewed the land ownership for the location you have provided. This area is owned by Transport for London and the Greater London Authority. However, it is a publicly accessible alley and is not part of the operational DLR site. The clearance of litter is the responsibility of local councils. All boroughs are funded directly from central government to carry this out. Under Section 89(1) of the Environmental Protection Act (EPA), street cleaning on publicly accessible land, and the removal of large, fly-tipped items falls under the responsibility of the local council.This means the Royal Borough of Greenwich is responsible for the removal of fly‑tipped waste at this location. For your reference, the Royal Borough of Greenwich can be contacted directly at: Royal Borough of Greenwich The Woolwich Centre Wellington Street Woolwich SE18 6HQ”

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenwich High Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large bedframe dumped outside The Florence, 30th March
item.description:
This large bedframe has been dumped opposite The Florence on Brockwell passage. It has been there for at least three days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 131-133, Dulwich Road, Herne Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Requires making safe - Cutdown and replacement insatalled

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 737, Old Kent Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
